page: Amazon Web Services c7a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 192 physical cores and 192 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, supported by the Nitro hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in both single-core and multi-core CPU execution, with stress-ng scores placing the server in the top 10% of tested systems. The AMD EPYC architecture, combined with DDR5 memory, delivers high memory bandwidth, particularly for small block cached reads at 24,745.29 GB/sec and large block uncached reads at 694.5 GB/sec. Extended CPU instruction tests, encryption, and physics simulations also rank in the top 10%. However, single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression perform in the average tier.

This server is highly optimized for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput static web serving, all of which achieved top-tier benchmark results. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM inference prompt processing for small and medium models. The primary tradeoff is the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU accelerators, which limits its suitability for tasks requiring local high-speed disk I/O or heavy GPU-bound parallel processing. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly cost-efficient for raw CPU-bound processing rather than memory-intensive database hosting.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core, with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps and no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and ray tracing, alongside high memory bandwidth. While single-threaded compression tasks perform in the average tier, the server is highly efficient for raw compute workloads. It is best suited for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and software compilation performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 192 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-core-count, compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive parallel processing.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 cores operating at 3.7 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, software compilation, and ray tracing performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
